description: Subsystem scope manifest for go-ethereum. Maps each L1 attack-surface layer to a scip-go package pattern set. Used by the T2 multi-scoped-run harness to produce one SCIP index per subsystem.

usage.note: scip-go supports native scoped indexing via positional PackagePatterns (see Round 5 research). Multiple patterns can be passed in one invocation.

notes.t2_composition: For a T2 whole-client audit, iterate over scopes and spawn one /plamen l1 run per scope with SUBSYSTEM_SCOPE set. Merge the N resulting SARIF reports with plamen_l1.sarif_merge.
notes.overlap: Note that eth/protocols/snap appears in both 'p2p' and 'state-sync' scopes. The SARIF merge harness deduplicates findings by (rule_id, uri, start_line, end_line) so this overlap does not produce double-reporting.
notes.coverage_check: Before running T2, verify the patterns cover all security-critical packages. Use 'scip-go --help' to see pattern syntax. Packages not in any scope will be missed by the T2 run — keep this manifest current with go-ethereum's upstream directory layout.
